TSLA: Cybercab spotted on Austin highway as Tesla moves into nighttime testing. A Tesla Cybercab prototype has been spotted testing on Austin's MoPac Expressway at night, indicating an aggressive push into nighttime highway travel for the autonomous vehicle. This advanced testing follows earlier sightings on busy South Austin streets and precedes a planned 2026 production timeline. The move to highway testing, especially at night, highlights the challenges of autonomous driving and Tesla's reliance on camera-only systems.

Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares Options Spot-On: On January 7th, 84,072 Contracts Were Traded, With 1.14 Million Open Interest. On January 7th, Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL.US) saw active options trading, with a total of 84,072 contracts traded. Call options represented 67.68% of transactions, while put options accounted for 32.32%. The open interest for TSLL reached approximately 1.14 million contracts, which is 93.02% of the average over the past 30 trading days.

Qualcomm to provide infotainment tech for new Volkswagen platform. Volkswagen plans to enter a long-term supply deal with U.S. chip designer Qualcomm to power the infotainment system of its new software platform. The deal, outlined in a letter of intent, makes Qualcomm the primary tech provider for a platform Volkswagen is developing with Rivian, with cars featuring this technology expected to launch in 2027. This partnership is crucial for Volkswagen to compete with rivals like BYD and Tesla in the growing market for software-defined vehicles.

Leo Michael cartoon: BYD dethrones Tesla for global EV crown. A cartoon by Leo Michael depicts BYD surpassing Tesla to become the top global electric vehicle seller. The article highlights this shift, emphasizing BYD's new position as the leader in the worldwide EV market.
TGT: Walmart Inc. $WMT Shares Acquired by Benjamin Edwards Inc.. Benjamin Edwards Inc. substantially increased its stake in Walmart (WMT) by 9.4% in Q3, making it a significant holding in their portfolio. This move aligns with a broadly positive analyst sentiment, with several firms raising price targets and a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating. Walmart's recent financial performance exceeded expectations, reinforcing the optimistic outlook for the company.

Target adds 2 Twin Cities wellness brands in 30% expansion - Bizwomen. Target is expanding its wellness offerings by 30% in 2026, adding two Twin Cities-based brands: So Good So You, a health beverage manufacturer, and Seeq Supply Inc., a protein powder company. This marks the second consecutive year of a significant rollout of wellness-related products for the retail giant. Both brands will have their products available in Target stores nationwide.

Target adds 2 Twin Cities wellness brands in 30% expansion - Bizwomen. Target (NYSE: TGT) is expanding its wellness product offerings by 30% in 2026, adding two Twin Cities-based brands: health beverage manufacturer So Good So You and protein powder company Seeq Supply Inc. So Good So You, known for its organic juice shots, will be available in nearly all 2,000 Target stores nationwide, including a new Target-exclusive sparkling beverage. Seeq, specializing in clear protein powder, will also see its products distributed across the country and online through the retailer.

Target adds 2 Twin Cities wellness brands in 30% expansion - Bizwomen. Target Corp. is expanding its wellness offerings by 30% in 2026, adding two Twin Cities-based brands: health beverage manufacturer So Good So You and protein powder company Seeq Supply Inc. This marks the second consecutive year of significant wellness product rollouts for the retailer. So Good So You's juice shots will be available in nearly all Target stores nationwide, and Seeq's clear protein powder will also be broadly accessible.

78 arrests made during undercover operations at Target, Walmart stores in North Carolina. Gastonia police conducted "Operation Naughty List" from November 28 to December 19, 2025, targeting retail theft at Target and Walmart stores. The undercover operation resulted in 78 arrests and 154 charges, including larceny and drug offenses. Authorities recovered approximately $4,300 worth of merchandise and seized illegal drugs.
SBUX: Jim Cramer on Starbucks: "I Think This Is the Year It Comes Back". Jim Cramer expressed optimism about Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX), noting green shoots and a potential rebound after past performance issues and operational challenges. He believes this year could mark a significant turnaround for the coffee giant, citing improvements in store operations and throughput. Despite acknowledging Starbucks' potential, the article suggests certain AI stocks may offer greater upside.

Spicy news. Chippewa Twp. preparing for new Chipotle Mexican Grill. Chippewa Township is experiencing a development boom in early 2026 with a new Chipotle Mexican Grill nearing completion and a Starbucks also under construction on Constitution Boulevard. This new Chipotle is located near a recently opened Sheetz, across from the Eat 'n Park in Chippewa Town Center. Additionally, land is being cleared for a new Diehl Kia dealership moving from Beaver Falls.
DIS: Disney, Webtoon join forces to launch new digital comics platform. The Walt Disney Company and Naver's Webtoon Entertainment have partnered to create a new digital comics platform. This platform will feature over 35,000 titles from Disney's portfolio, including Marvel and Star Wars, alongside Webtoon's original content, offering both traditional and vertical-scroll formats. The collaboration also involved Disney taking a 2% equity stake in Webtoon Entertainment.

TIME Names Disney Among ‘America’s Most Iconic Companies’. TIME magazine has recognized The Walt Disney Company as one of "America's Most Iconic Companies" for its enduring impact on the nation's economic and cultural landscape. CEO Bob Iger highlighted Disney's century-long role in American storytelling, bringing magic and innovation to global audiences. The company is also celebrating America's semiquincentennial with special programming and experiences across its brands.
NKE: U.S. stocks mixed at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.55%. U.S. stocks showed mixed performance on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising 0.55% and the S&P 500 gaining 0.01%, while the NASDAQ Composite fell 0.44%. Gains in Oil & Gas, Consumer Goods, and Consumer Services sectors offset losses in Technology, Healthcare, and Utilities. Notable performers included Nike, Home Depot, and Sherwin-Williams on the Dow, while NVIDIA and Amgen saw declines.

NIKE (NYSE:NKE) Coverage Initiated at Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft. Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft has initiated coverage on NIKE (NYSE:NKE) with a "Hold" rating, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $75.45. This comes as NIKE reported strong quarterly earnings, beating EPS and revenue estimates, and notable insider purchases were made by CEO Elliott Hill and Director Tim Cook. The article details recent analyst ratings, share performance, and insider trading activities, alongside outlining key positive, neutral, and negative market sentiments affecting the company.
